docs.title: Intro
docs.text:
Warning

THIS WEBSITE IS UNDER CONSTRUCTRION! EVERYTHING IS SUBJECT TO CHANGE!

An all in one solution for handling 2d pixel art characters in the Unity Game Engine.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Character Piece Colletion
docs.text:
A Scriptable Object that acts as a layer for a Layered Character Type. It contains a list of all valid sprites that can be used for that layer of the character.

Warning

A Character Piece Collection with an empty Character Pieces list will be invalid!

docs.title: Fields
docs.text:
Field Description Attached Character Type The Character Type the Collection is meant for Collection Name A name for the collection used when displaying collection Character Piece Asset Label The label used to finds sprites for the collection Include NA Option Include a blank option at the start of the Character Pieces list

Note

Character Pieces list must be refreshed after toggling Include NA Option

docs.title: Buttons
docs.text: Button Description Get Character Pieces Finds all sprites using the assigned label Clear Character Piece Clears the Character Pieces list

docs.title: Character Type Base
docs.text: The CharacterTypeBaseSO class defines a base configuration asset for all character types.

docs.title: CharacterTypeID
docs.text:
• Type: string
  • Attributes: [SerializeField], [Tooltip], [BoxGroup("Settings")]
  • Description: A unique identifier used to reference this character type. Must be unique across all character types.

docs.title: BaseSpritesheet
docs.text:
• Type: Sprite
  • Attributes: [SerializeField], [Tooltip], [BoxGroup("Settings")]
  • Description: Defines the required base sprite sheet for the character type. Set this spritesheets Sprite Mode to Multiple and slice it. Whenever this character is used the sprites in the BaseSpritesheet will be used and a shader will then override them with the finalized character. Character spritesheets with mismatched dimensions will be rejected when validated.

docs.title: CharacterController
docs.text:
• Type: RuntimeAnimatorController
  • Attributes: [SerializeField], [BoxGroup("Settings")]
  • Description: The animator controller asset assigned to characters of this type. Animations inside this controller should use sprites in the BaseSpritesheet.

docs.title: IsValidCharacterSpriteSheet(Sprite sprite) → bool
docs.text: Checks if a given sprite matches the width and height of the BaseSpritesheet. - Returns: true if the sprite size matches; otherwise false.

docs.title: Character Types
docs.text:
Character Types are Scriptable Objects that define core aspects of a character. They are the heart of the Character Management System.

Note

All Characters Require a Character Type.

docs.title: Character Type Base
docs.text: All Character Types inherit from CharacterTypeBaseSO, which contains the core properties shared across all Character Types.

docs.title: Character Type Variants
docs.text: Variant Modularity Runtime Customization Best For Unified Single spritesheet None Pre-rendered, fixed characters Layered Layered spritesheets High Modular, editable characters

docs.title: 1. Unified Character Type
docs.text:
Each character uses a single spritesheet containing the fully assembled character. No runtime customization is possible. - Use Case: Characters with fixed, pre-rendered appearances. - Example: Simplistic characters where their appearance is pre-determined and won't need to be changed.

Read More → Unified Character Type

docs.title: 2. Layered Character Type
docs.text:
A set of separate spritesheets, each containing one visual layer of the character. - Use Case: Customizable player characters or dynamically generated NPCs. - Example: Body, Outfit, Hairstyle, Accessory

Read More → Layered Character Type

docs.title: Layered Character Type
docs.text:
LayeredCharacterTypeSO Inherits from CharacterTypeBaseSO.

Note

Layered Character Types must be placed inside a Resources folder in order to be valid!

docs.title: Base Character Type Fields
docs.text: Fields Description Character Type ID A Unique Identifer Base Spritesheet The default character spritesheet Character Controller The Animator Controller used

docs.title: Character Creator Settings (Unfinished)
docs.text: Fields Description Use Clean Character Piece Names Replaces underscores with spaces when display Character Piece Names Character Preview Controller The Animator Controller to use when previewing a character in the Character Creator Character Placeholder Sprite Default sprite to show representing this Character Type Base Character Piece Collection Default Character Piece Collection to be used

docs.title: Character Piece Collections
docs.text: A list of Character Piece Collections

docs.title: Unified Character Type
docs.text: UnifiedCharacterTypeSO Inherits from CharacterTypeBaseSO.

docs.title: Base Character Type Fields
docs.text: Field Description Character Type ID A Unique Identifer Base Spritesheet The default character spritesheet Character Controller The Animator Controller used

docs.title: Creating a New Unifed Character Type
docs.text: To create a Unified Character Type right click the Project window and navigate to Create > BlazerTech Character Management System > Unified Character Type

docs.title: Base Spritesheet
docs.text:
The default Spritesheet used for a Character Type.

Set this Spritesheets Sprite Mode to Multiple and slice it. Anytime a character is used this Spritesheet will be used, a shader will then override it to show the finalized character.

docs.title: Character Controller
docs.text: The Character Controller is the Animator Controller that all characters of the same Character Type will use. You can set it up however you want for your game. The only requirement is all sprites used in the animations within the Animator Controller must be sprites from the Base Character Spritesheet

docs.title: Character Type ID
docs.text:
The Character Type ID is a unique identifier for a Character Type and is Required.

Warning

If a Character Type ID is either not set or is the same as another Character Type, the Character Type will be invalid!

docs.title: Code example
docs.text:
An example of a codeblock for Python:

add_numbers.py
# Function to add two numbers
def add_two_numbers(num1, num2):
    return num1 + num2

#example usage
result = add_two_numbers(5, 3)
print('The sum is', result)
